mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening expectations, driving negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yield positive 20-day forward returns in over 58% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening real economic activity that supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion periods, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal robust consumer confidence and construction sector demand, which translates into positive equity market performance over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.5% triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day returns in over 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.0% indicates weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, prompting portfolio de-risking that generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth below 1.0% is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.
